---
title: "nitrix.metrics.preprocess"
generated: true
signature-hash: 4697ee4ad630b19c
description: "Intensity preprocessing for registration (the fMRIPrep front-end)."
listing:
  id: nitrix-metrics-preprocess-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-metrics-preprocess/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.metrics.preprocess`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Intensity preprocessing for registration (the fMRIPrep front-end).

The two within-/cross-modality conditioning steps an `antsRegistration` run
applies before the metric sees the data:

- [`winsorize`](nitrix-metrics-preprocess/winsorize.qmd) -- clip each image to its low/high intensity percentiles
  (`--winsorize-image-intensities [0.005, 0.995]`), so outliers (hot voxels,
  skull, artifacts) do not dominate an SSD / Demons force.  Also relieves the
  step-normalisation global-outlier sensitivity (the clipped image has no
  extreme voxel to set the trust-region cap).
- [`match_histogram`](nitrix-metrics-preprocess/match_histogram.qmd) -- remap the moving image's intensity distribution onto
  the reference's by CDF / quantile transport (`--use-histogram-matching 1`), so
  a within-modality SSD / CC metric is not fighting a global intensity
  gain/offset.

Both are pure and differentiable (clip + sort-based quantiles + linear
interpolation), so they compose with the differentiable-layer story.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-metrics-preprocess-symbols}
:::
